arts.ttl: West Brom will soon unleash wildcard who 'wants Tony Mowbray to shout at him'
arts.sum: Bany was unveiled to the Albion supporters ahead of the victory over Sheffield Wednesday at The Hawthorns last Saturday and, while there is no time pressure to integrate him into the first-team squad, Tony Mowbray has a positive first impression of the 21-year-old, who he says 'wants the coach to shout at him' in order to demand more. "He's like a number ten, he wants to have the ball," Mowbray said.

arts.ttl: Tony Mowbray makes West Brom promise to 'really exciting' Tottenham star Will Lankshear
arts.sum: Tony Mowbray likes what he has seen so far from Will Lankshear in a West Bromwich Albion shirt so far, as the young loanee pushes for a first start when the Baggies face Millwall in need of a response on Saturday (kick off 3pm). Tony Mowbray greenlit a loan move for Tottenham Hotspur striker Will Lankshear before West Brom's transfer window ended

arts.ttl: West Brom boss expects fired-up Millwall side following ‘convincing’ Plymouth defeat
arts.sum: West Brom boss Tony Mowbray has stated he expects an exciting game as his side visits the Den for their Championship clash tomorrow. The Lions enter the match after a 5-1 humbling at the hands of Plymouth Argyle, who were dead last in the Championship going into the game.

arts.ttl: TV channel and date set for Crystal Palace v Millwall FA Cup clash
arts.sum: Crystal Palace will host South London rivals Millwall in the fourth round of the FA Cup at 12:15 GMT on Saturday, 1st March. “It’s going to be a great game and hopefully we can continue the cup run.

arts.ttl: FA Cup fifth-round TV schedule announced as BBC and ITV both get huge matches
arts.sum: Kick-off times and TV schedules for the eight FA Cup fifth-round matches have been confirmed, with holders Manchester United and 2023 winner Manchester City both involved

arts.ttl: Mihailo Ivanovic exclusive interview as he talks about rapport with Millwall fans, idolising Mitrovic and why price tag is a ‘good pressure’
arts.sum: The 20-year-old has scored five goals for the South London club, four of those in the Championship, including back-to-back winners at Luton and Portsmouth at the end of last month which really kicked the Alex Neil reign into life. They supported me and they didn’t know then how I was going to play and how I am as a person.”
